title: RabbitMQ Memory Alarm
spl:
index=messaging sourcetype="rabbitmq:node"
| where mem_alarm==true OR mem_used/mem_limit > 0.75
| table _time, name, mem_used, mem_limit, mem_alarm
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw logs or metrics.
description: `mem_alarm` blocks publishers when `vm_memory_high_watermark` is hit. Early warning from `memory` and `allocated` fields.
value: `mem_alarm` blocks publishers when `vm_memory_high_watermark` is hit. Early warning from `memory` and `allocated` fields.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for signs that blocks publishers when is hit.
implementation: Poll nodes every minute. Alert at 75% memory or alarm true. Flow control from alarm requires immediate consumer scale-up or queue purge policy.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€˘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: RabbitMQ management API `/api/nodes`.
â€˘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `mem_used`, `mem_limit`, `mem_alarm`.
â€˘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 â€” Configure data collection
Poll nodes every minute. Alert at 75% memory or alarm true. Flow control from alarm requires immediate consumer scale-up or queue purge policy.

Step 2 â€” Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=messaging sourcetype="rabbitmq:node"
| where mem_alarm==true OR mem_used/mem_limit > 0.75
| table _time, name, mem_used, mem_limit, mem_alarm
```

Understanding this SPL

**RabbitMQ Memory Alarm** â€” `mem_alarm` blocks publishers when `vm_memory_high_watermark` is hit. Early warning from `memory` and `allocated` fields.

Documented **Data sources**: `mem_used`, `mem_limit`, `mem_alarm`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): RabbitMQ management API `/api/nodes`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedâ€”rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: messaging; **sourcetype**: rabbitmq:node. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

â€˘ Scopes the data: index=messaging, sourcetype="rabbitmq:node". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
â€˘ Filters the current rows with `where mem_alarm==true OR mem_used/mem_limit > 0.75` â€” typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
â€˘ Pipeline stage (see **RabbitMQ Memory Alarm**): table _time, name, mem_used, mem_limit, mem_alarm


Step 3 â€” Validate
Compare with the broker or gatewayâ€™s own UI or CLI (`kafka-consumer-groups`, RabbitMQ management, ActiveMQ console, or Traefik/Envoy access log on the node) for the same period.


Step 4 â€” Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (memory % per node), Line chart (mem_used trend), Table (nodes in alarm).
visualization: Gauge (memory % per node), Line chart (mem_used trend), Table (nodes in alarm).
